arguments.task: Research PitchBook using only public official or first-party pages. Find evidence for: product description, any API or developer docs, authentication method details if public, pricing/access gating or contact-sales language, rate limit notes if any, and whether PitchBook has an official MCP server or first-party GitHub registry presence. Prioritize pages on pitchbook.com and official docs/support/help centers. Also check if the site search or footer links reveal API/developer resources. Return a concise list of findings with exact page titles and URLs visited. If pages are gated or login-only, note that explicitly and do not sign in.
On official PitchBook pages, find any pricing, plans, demo request, contact sales, free trial, or login-gated messaging relevant to access. Return exact quotes and URLs from official pages only.

Search official PitchBook web properties for API, developer, documentation, SSO, authentication, and rate limit information. Return exact URLs and short findings. If only support/help pages or login requirements appear, note that.

Check for first-party official GitHub organization or repository for PitchBook related to API, developer tools, or MCP server. Also check the GitHub MCP registry if relevant. Return only first-party or clearly official findings with URLs; if none found, say no official first-party GitHub/MCP evidence found.

input.thought: Run parallel browser research tasks across official PitchBook sources and first-party GitHub presence.

arguments.task: Read the official PitchBook Direct API product page and extract exact public statements about API type/protocol, data format, endpoints or capabilities, and any public mention of authentication, developer docs, or rate limits. Return exact quotes with URL. If no auth or rate-limit details are publicly visible, state that clearly.

On official PitchBook Help Center pages, search for SSO, API, Direct Data, authentication, and rate limits. Return any exact quotes and URLs from public help articles. If search or articles require login or cannot be found publicly, say so explicitly.

Verify the public official GitHub organization for PitchBook and list public repositories. Determine whether any repository is an official MCP server or developer/API toolkit. Also verify whether the official modelcontextprotocol servers registry lists PitchBook. Return exact URLs and concise findings only.

raw_result.description: Private capital market intelligence platform with company, deal, fund, and investor data plus direct data access products.
raw_result.auth_notes: Public login flow redirects to Morningstar login with OAuth2-style parameters in the URL (`protocol=oauth2`, `response_type=code`, redirect callback), and the sign-in page publicly shows both email/password login and "Sign in with SSO". Public evidence does not verify API auth specifically, only application login/auth options.
raw_result.gating_reason: PitchBook pricing and API-related product access are sales-gated. Official pages use request-pricing/request-trial/request-direct-data flows and do not expose self-serve API signup.

api_surface.breadth: Official public product pages describe a PitchBook Direct API for on-demand retrieval of PitchBook data and indicate JSON/RESTful access, but public documentation for endpoints, auth specifics, and operational details was not verified from the gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Gathered evidence indicates the official `pitchbook` GitHub organization has no relevant public API/MCP repository beyond an unrelated public repo; no official PitchBook MCP server was verified from gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Public marketing pages confirm an API exists, but developer docs, API authentication specifics, and likely customer-gated access were not publicly verified; building would require sales-led access and/or customer documentation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public PitchBook rate-limit documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official pricing page says pricing varies by seats, firm type, and premium offerings such as Direct Data or CRM Integration, with a request-pricing CTA. Free-trial access is qualification-based ("we’ll see if a free trial might be right for you"). Public API page is product-marketing oriented rather than developer-documentation oriented. Login is clearly gated and includes SSO.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several required fields remain partially unverified from public evidence already gathered: API auth method is inferred from app login rather than confirmed for the API; REST/JSON characterization and lack of MCP are based on browser-task summaries rather than direct captured doc quotes in this turn; official rate-limit and endpoint documentation were not publicly verified.
